description_long: This object consists of three mushrooms with large, rounded caps that are a deep red color, each adorned with numerous irregular white patches scattered across the surface. The caps are smooth and slightly glossy, with the largest mushroom positioned centrally and two smaller ones clustered nearby. The stems are short, thick, and have a fibrous, grayish-brown appearance, supporting the caps above the ground. The white patches on the caps are unevenly distributed, giving the mushrooms a distinctive, almost warty look. The gills beneath the caps are not visible in any view, and the mushrooms appear to be growing directly from an undefined, neutral surface. The overall form and coloration are reminiscent of the iconic Amanita muscaria, often found in natural or park-like urban settings.
description: A cluster of three red-capped mushrooms with white, warty patches and thick, fibrous stems. The caps are smooth and glossy, with no visible gills underneath.
description_view_0: The largest mushroom is prominent, with its red cap and white patches clearly visible, and two smaller mushrooms are grouped to the right.
description_view_1: The central large mushroom is flanked by two smaller mushrooms, all showing their caps with scattered white patches and thick stems.
description_view_2: The view highlights the side of the largest mushroom and the cap of one smaller mushroom, emphasizing the white patches and stem textures.
description_view_3: The largest mushroom cap dominates the view, with its red surface and white patches most visible, and the stem is centered.
attribute_cap_pattern: red with irregular white warty patches
